rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court is to determine whether future accounts should be waived and whether bond should be exonerated. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE DORIS BARKSDALE REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. remote appearance highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. ISSUES (to be addressed at hearing): 1) Cora Lewis has not filed notice. Cora Lewis has not provided a proposed order to be relieved as council. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.] 2) Court to determine why there has been no filed response to the Examiner's Notes. a. The Petition is not verified by the fiduciary. All accounts, petitions and other pleadings made in his or her official capacity must be personally signed and verified. (LRSF 14.18). b. The ending balance of cash in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. (LRSF 14.62). c. Please provide timesheets in support of the request for trustee fees. (LRSF 14.89). d. On Schedule C please explain the Residential Facility Fees entries and why it appears to only be for 3 months. e. On Schedule C please explain under Caregiver Expenses 12/28/17 Octavia Holborough court-ordered guardian fee $975.00 and provide a receipt. f. Please explain why on 02/12/18 Olive Reid was paid $1,1524.15 and on 02/12/18 Octavia Holborough was paid $1,209.15 was caregiving expenses. g. On Schedule C please explain entry 05/31/17 Carolyn Ozonsi conservatorship fees of $12,000.00. h. On Schedule C please explain entry 09/05/17 Franchesca Callejo legal fees of $220.00. i. A hard copy of the proposed order must be dropped off at the Superior Court in room 202. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF HERBERT VILLAFLORES ARCEO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/03/2020 (no earlier date available). 1) No statement of the US Bank savings account showing the balance as of 03/27/2019 is on file. 2) The beginning balance of the US Bank personal account is different from the amount reported on the inventory and appraisal (filed 07/24/2019). 3) No statements of the Chevron employee saving investment account have been filed for any period. 4) Schedule A reported interest paid to the US Bank savings account on 03/14/2019 which predates the petitioner's appointment. 5) No supplemental inventory has been filed to report the $80,000 inheritance, nor is a GC-042 form on file. See Probate Code sections 2610(a) & 2613. 6) Why did the petitioner pay expenses of the conservatee's unsupervised trust from the conservatorship estate? 7) Receipts for the assets transferred to the successor conservator, other than the Wells Fargo CD & Bank of America checking funds, must be filed. 8) A statement required by CRC 7.751(b) & 7.702 must be filed for the petitioner and his attorneys respectively. Each factor under CRC 7.756 must also be addressed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTINE ANN MITCHELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights Of Leslie Isaac
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) Petitioner requests an order determining that Decedent's father, Barry Mitchell, is disinherited pursuant to Probate Code 6452(1)(3) and that Decedent's surviving siblings, Leslie Isaac (petitioner) and Clark Isaac are the persons entitled to the assets of this estate. It is noted that at least partial payment of child support was paid prior to Decedent attaining the age of majority and that no allegation has been made as to how Petitioner has knowledge that no communication was made by Barry Mitchell from the time Decedent turned seven to her attaining the age of majority. 2) Petitioner has not verified the Petition. Attorney Randall Boessenecker has verified it and alleges that she resides outside the county in which he has his office. 3) Notice of Hearing was served on Barry Mitchell at two different addresses, both being P.O. Boxes. Remote appearances are highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 6th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARITAL TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Confirming Appointment Of Successor Trustees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/27/2020 (no earlier date available). A supplement addressing the following issue must be filed: Paragraph 8 of the will authorizes appointment of successor trustee in the event of the inability or unwillingness of "both of the above named individuals" to act. The petitioners have already been appointed as successor trustees as a result of the inability or unwillingness of "both of the above named individuals" to act. Does paragraph 8 give the petitioners authority to nominate successor trustees in the event of their own inability or unwillingness to act?
